_meta.purpose: Surface every failure of the audit pipeline. Strict variant (action='consent.*' / 'alert.*' / 'patient.delete' / 'dsr.*' etc.) blocking writes is a B-09 SLO violation and must page the on-call. Best-effort variant elevation indicates a Postgres or PostgREST regression.
fields_emitted: requestId (optional, threaded through from the strict path)
_meta.import_to: Datadog Dashboards → New → Import dashboard JSON

title: Uelfy — AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED
description: Audit-pipeline failures by variant + action. Strict-variant non-zero ⇒ page on-call (SEV-1 invariant breach, see SLOs).

monitors._purpose: B-09 SLO: zero strict-variant audit failures per 30 days. Page immediately.
monitors.name: Uelfy — strict AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED occurred

monitors._purpose: Best-effort baseline alarm — non-zero rate over 1h is a soft-failure signal.
monitors.name: Uelfy — best-effort AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED rate elevated
monitors.message:
Best-effort audit pipeline failing >5/h — expect: Postgres connectivity wobble, PostgREST regression, or PostgREST policy mismatch. SEV-3.

Runbook: docs/27-INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md §11.2
